methods.description: Statistical analysis to determine the lateral and vertical distribution of microplastics (MPs) in sediment samples using PERMANOVA and Kruskal-Wallis tests.

steps.description: Results of the shape and size of MPs were expressed as mean ± standard deviation of MPs/Kg dry weight of sediment (DW).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean and standard deviation of MPs per Kg dry weight of sediment

steps.description: Permutation Multivariate Analysis of Variance (PERMANOVA) was used to test for the lateral distribution of the different shapes of MPs using the factor Position as fixed factor.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PERMANOVA to test lateral distribution of MPs

steps.description: The vertical stratification in the three sections of the cores was tested using PERMANOVA and the same statistical design described above.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PERMANOVA to test vertical stratification of MPs

steps.description: The vertical stratification and the lateral distribution of MPs in the different sampling points were analysed using the non-parametric Kruskal-Wallis test due to the non-normal distribution of the data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis test for MPs distribution analysis

steps.description: In case of significant results, data were further analysed using the Dunn test for multiple comparisons with a Bonferroni correction method.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Dunn test with Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons

steps.description: Statistical analysis were performed using the R environment (version 3.5.1) in RStudio (Version 1.1.463).
steps.searchable_summary: use R and RStudio for statistical analysis

methods.description: Procedures for processing sediment core samples to extract and identify microplastics (MPs) using density separation and visual identification methods.

steps.description: Sediment core samples were extruded using a wooden plunger and subsampled using a metal knife into 5 cm sections and placed in aluminium containers.
steps.searchable_summary: extrude and subsample sediment cores into sections

steps.description: Samples were then dried at 50 °C for 48 hours or until dry.
steps.searchable_summary: dry sediment samples at 50 °C

steps.description: Samples were homogenized and sieved with a stainless-steel sieve (mesh size: 3 mm) and the retained particles were sorted.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize and sieve sediment samples

steps.description: MPs <3 mm were then extracted by means of a slightly modified version of Thompson et al. (2004) density separation method.
steps.searchable_summary: perform density separation to extract MPs

steps.description: From each sample 200 g of sieved sediment was placed in a glass jar to which 150 ml H2O2 (30%) was added for organic matter oxidation.
steps.searchable_summary: add H2O2 for organic matter oxidation

steps.description: Samples were placed in a water bath to cool down overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: cool samples in water bath overnight

steps.description: Samples were swirled to enhance organic matter oxidation.
steps.searchable_summary: swirl samples to enhance oxidation

steps.description: 250 ml of saturated NaCl solution was added, jars were shaken vigorously and left to settle for at least 4 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: add NaCl solution and shake samples

steps.description: The resulting supernatant, with floating particles, was filtered using a vacuum pump and Whatman GF/F filter.
steps.searchable_summary: filter supernatant with vacuum pump

steps.description: To ensure that all particles were extracted, the density separation step was repeated twice.
steps.searchable_summary: repeat density separation twice

steps.description: Particles retained in the sieves and in the filter were analysed following the method described by Hildalgo-Ruz et al. (2012) and Marine & Environmental Research Institute (2012) with minor alterations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze particles using Hildalgo-Ruz method

steps.description: MPs were counted using a stereoscope with a 10x and 20x magnification and classified as fragment, fiber, pellet or sheet.
steps.searchable_summary: count and classify MPs using stereoscope

steps.description: In cases of difficult identification between organic matter and plastic, particles were exposed to the hot needle test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform hot needle test for identification

steps.description: If after this test a conclusion still remained uncertain, particles were removed and placed under a microscope with 40x magnification for further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: use microscope for further particle analysis

steps.description: Measures to avoid contamination were adopted.
steps.searchable_summary: implement contamination prevention measures
